commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Release owners need to decide the manual publishing policy and whether the final-head local proof is sufficient for a workflow that controls release assets.; Cleared: The diff narrows release publishing behavior and validates tag-like values; no new concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the changed workflow. (sha=2cd04f0b600b30358bf989c035e51b82c6744ebe)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Release CLI workflow so manual dispatches upload artifacts only, release publishing and Homebrew tap updates run only on release events, and release tag values are validated before shell use.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows a manual tag can be supplied while checkout remains on the selected workflow ref, and the release upload plus tap jobs run when that tag input is nonempty; a destructive live repro against real release assets should not be run.
commands.review_followup: Release owners need to decide the manual publishing policy and whether the final-head local proof is sufficient for a workflow that controls release assets.

target.title: Harden Release CLI manual dispatch
target.body:
Fixes #1153.

## Summary

- make manual `Release CLI` dispatches artifact-only, even when `tag` is provided
- preserve no-tag manual artifact dispatches from branch refs like `main`
- keep GitHub release asset uploads limited to `release` events
- keep the Homebrew tap update limited to `release` events
- validate release tag values only for release events or manual runs with a tag
- pass release tag/request id values through environment variables before shell use
- clarify that the manual `tag` input packages artifacts but does not publish them

## Why

The current workflow lets a manual dispatch build from the selected workflow ref while using `inputs.tag` as `RELEASE_TAG`. Because the upload step runs when `inputs.tag != ''` and uses `gh release upload "$TAG" ... --clobber`, a manual run can publish assets for a release tag from a different checkout ref.

ClawSweeper validated #1153 as source-repro, P1, impact:security, and fix-shape-clear. It also noted that a live repro against the real repo should not be run because it would mutate release assets.

This PR keeps the narrow policy fix: manual runs build workflow artifacts only; publishing remains tied to release events. The follow-up shell hardening validates tag-like values before release/tap use and moves the tag/request id into step environment variables.

Codex and ClawSweeper later caught that validating every `RELEASE_TAG` broke no-tag manual artifact builds, because branch refs like `main` do not match the tag allowlist. The final head fixes that by running release-tag validation only for release events or manual dispatches where `inputs.tag` is provided.

## Proof

Issue proof:

- #1153 has ClawSweeper `clawsweeper:source-repro`, `P1`, `impact:security`, `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`, and `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`.
- ClawSweeper's review says source inspection gives a high-confidence path: manual dispatch supplies `inputs.tag`, checkout remains on the workflow ref, and upload publishes to that tag with `--clobber`.

Patch proof at final head:

```text
$ ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-cli.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'
yaml ok

$ rg -n "if: github.event_name == 'release' \\|\\| inputs.tag != ''|if: github.event_name == 'release'$|if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'$|RELEASE_TAG:|REQUEST_ID:" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
48:      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ inputs.tag || github.ref_name }}
73:        if: github.event_name == 'release' || inputs.tag != ''
228:        if: github.event_name == 'release'
240:        if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
251:    if: github.event_name == 'release'
256:          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ github.ref_name }}
275:          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ steps.release.outputs.tag }}
276:          REQUEST_ID: ${{ steps.release.outputs.request_id }}
304:          REQUEST_ID: ${{ steps.release.outputs.request_id }}

$ rg -n 'tag="\$\{\{ inputs\.tag|tag="\$\{\{ steps\.release\.outputs|request_id="\$\{\{ steps\.release\.outputs' .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
# no matches

$ ./final-head-validation-simulation
validation-skipped event=workflow_dispatch input='' ref=main tag=main
validation-skipped event=workflow_dispatch input='' ref=fix/release-cli-tag-provenance tag=fix/release-cli-tag-provenance
validate-ok event=workflow_dispatch input=v-proof-1154 ref=fix/release-cli-tag-provenance tag=v-proof-1154
validate-reject event=workflow_dispatch input=v1.2\$\(echo\ bad\) ref=fix/release-cli-tag-provenance tag=v1.2\$\(echo\ bad\)
validate-ok event=release input='' ref=v0.29.2 tag=v0.29.2
validate-reject event=release input='' ref=main tag=main

$ REQUEST_ID=codexbar-test gh api repos/steipete/CodexBar --jq 'env.REQUEST_ID'
codexbar-test

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Live fork proof from the first patch commit:

I previously manually dispatched the patched `Release CLI` workflow on my fork with a harmless proof tag before the tag-validation follow-up:

```text
$ gh workflow run release-cli.yml --repo jskoiz/CodexBar --ref fix/release-cli-tag-provenance -f tag=v-proof-1154
https://github.com/jskoiz/CodexBar/actions/runs/26432040743
```

Run result:

- event: `workflow_dispatch`
- branch: `fix/release-cli-tag-provenance`
- commit: `45e2ad1dd5b16036e6b088de49970cb00a291c91`
- conclusion: `success`
- `update-homebrew-tap`: `skipped`

Relevant job-step results from that run:

```text
macos-x86_64: Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-x64:    Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
macos-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
```

Artifacts were uploaded for all four targets:

```text
codexbar-cli-macos-x86_64
codexbar-cli-linux-x64
codexbar-cli-linux-arm64
codexbar-cli-macos-arm64
```

One downloaded artifact confirmed the manual tag was packaged as an artifact rather than published as a release asset:

```text
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz.sha256
```

I did not rerun the workflow after the tag-validation follow-up to avoid spending macOS runner minutes. The final-head change is covered by local YAML parsing, targeted grep checks, behavior simulation for no-tag manual dispatches, tagged manual dispatches, and release events, plus the `gh --jq env.REQUEST_ID` check. All commits use `[skip ci]` to avoid implicit macOS CI on push/PR update. ClawSweeper may still rerun on the PR update.

If maintainers prefer preserving a manual repair-publish path, the alternate shape is to check out the requested tag, verify `HEAD` matches that tag before upload, validate the tag before any shell use, and avoid `--clobber` by default.
target.author: jskoiz

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.freeform_prompt: re-review\n\nAdded live fork workflow proof from https://github.com/jskoiz/CodexBar/actions/runs/26432040743 showing workflow_dispatch with tag=v-proof-1154 skipped release upload and Homebrew tap dispatch while uploading workflow artifacts for all four targets.
